text:
; and the remaining
Rasis viz. Mesa, Mithuna, Simha and Dhanus, are Land-Signs.
Karkataka, Vrscika and Mina are also termed Kitas or
Reptiles. This appellation, however, is mostly applied to Vrscika.
The haunts or abodes of these Signs are in order (1) Forest or
Plateau, (2) Field or Meadow, (3) Bed chamber or Village, (4)
Pond or pit with water, (5) Mountain cave, (6) Land with water
and vegetation, (7) Market-place or busy town, (8) Hole, (9) Battle-
field or where kings assemble. (10) Forest with plenty of water in
it, (11) Place frequented by potters, and (12) Water. Mesa,
Vrsabha, Sirhha, the latter half of Dhanus and the first half of
Makara are called Quadruped signs. Mithuna, Kanya, Tula,

GENERAL PRINCIPLES 9
the first half of Dhanus, and Kumbha are designated as Human
signs. Kitas have been already named. Thus you see that
4-i. Rasis come under the Human category, four Rasis (3 full
and two half signs) under the Quadruped, and three under the
Reptile category.
